# services.rshim

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.rshim`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.rshim.backend` | `null or one of "usb", "pcie", "pcie_lf"` | Specify the backend to attach. If not specified, the driver will scan all rshim backends unless the `device` option is given with a device name specified. |
| `services.rshim.config` | `attribute set of (signed integer or string)` | Structural setting for the rshim configuration file (`/etc/rshim.conf`). It can be used to specify the static mapping between rshim devices and rshim names. It can also be used to ignore some rshim devices. |
| `services.rshim.device` | `null or string` | Specify the device name to attach. The backend driver can be deduced from the device name, thus the `backend` option is not needed. |
| `services.rshim.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable user-space rshim driver for the BlueField SoC. |
| `services.rshim.index` | `null or signed integer` | Specify the index to create device path `/dev/rshim<index>`. It's also used to create network interface name `tmfifo_net<index>`. This option is needed when multiple rshim instances are running. |
| `services.rshim.log-level` | `integer between 0 and 4 (both inclusive)` | Specify the log level (0:none, 1:error, 2:warning, 3:notice, 4:debug). |
| `services.rshim.package` | `package` | The rshim-user-space package to use. |
